notes: Anonymous dimension block *D315 (DIM layer, DEFPOINTS points, DIMDOT arrowheads, MTEXT '5795') -- a linear dimension cache, not a wall.

rationale.evidence: All 9 entities sit on DIM/DEFPOINTS layers: two parallel witness LINEs (8C0E, 8C0F) and one perpendicular dimension LINE (8C10) frame a bbox whose long axis (5795 units, y from 27375.207 to 33170.207) matches the MTEXT '\A1;5795' text exactly. Two DIMDOT INSERTs (8C11, 8C12) sit at the line ends as arrowhead blocks, and three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8C14-8C16) mark the dimension's definition points.
rationale.rule: A *D-prefixed anonymous block name combined with DIM-layer LINE/INSERT geometry, DEFPOINTS POINT entities, and an MTEXT field code (\A1;<number>) matching the geometric span is the AutoCAD fingerprint of an associative linear dimension block, not architectural wall geometry, regardless of the raw line lengths involved.
rationale.counterfactual: If the LINE entities were on an architectural wall layer (e.g. A-WALL) instead of DIM, or if the DEFPOINTS points and DIMDOT arrowhead INSERTs were absent, the wall_likelihood judgment would rise substantially.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection cannot confirm whether this anonymous block is actually referenced by a live DIMENSION entity in the host layout or is an orphaned/unreferenced definition, since parent-layout usage is outside the block-definition projection.

DIMDOT INSERTs 8C1A/8C1B mark the arrowhead ends and three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8C1D-8C1F) are present, identical in structure to the adjacent *D315 dimension.
rationale.rule: A *D-prefixed anonymous block name combined with DIM-layer LINE/INSERT geometry, DEFPOINTS POINT entities, and an MTEXT field code matching the geometric span is the AutoCAD fingerprint of an associative linear dimension block, not architectural wall geometry.
rationale.counterfactual: Replacing the DIM/DEFPOINTS layers with a wall layer, or removing the DIMDOT arrowhead INSERTs and MTEXT text, would push this toward a wall/plan-fragment classification instead.
rationale.uncertainty: Cannot verify from this projection whether the DIM layer is frozen or non-plotting in the host drawing, or whether the block is currently referenced by an active DIMENSION entity.

notes: Anonymous dimension block *D320 (DIM layer, DEFPOINTS points, _ArchTick arrowheads, MTEXT '185') with a 5-LINE jog scaffold for a short 185-unit span -- a dimension cache, not a wall.

rationale.evidence: 11 entities on DIM/DEFPOINTS layers: horizontal LINEs 8C3B/8C3C are the witness lines, while short perpendicular jog LINEs 8C3D/8C3E and the connecting dimension LINE 8C3F form AutoCAD's standard 'shoulder' pattern used when a dimension line (here 185 units, matching MTEXT '\A1;185') is too short for text/ticks to fit inline. Two _ArchTick INSERTs (8C40/8C41, architectural tick marks rather than DIMDOT dots) and three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8C43-8C45) complete the block.
rationale.rule: The 5-LINE 'jog/shoulder' variant (vs. the 3-LINE baseline) still fingerprints as an associative dimension block -- it appears specifically when the MTEXT numeric value is small relative to the drawing scale, so extra LINEs extend the witness/dimension lines outward to fit arrowheads and text, not because extra wall segments exist.
rationale.counterfactual: If the two short perpendicular LINEs (8C3D, 8C3E) were part of a continuous wall outline rather than symmetric jogs centered on the dimension midpoint, or if DEFPOINTS/_ArchTick scaffolding were absent, this would read as wall geometry instead.
rationale.uncertainty: Cannot verify from this projection whether the _ArchTick tick-mark style (vs. DIMDOT elsewhere in this shard) reflects a genuine drawing-wide dimension-style change or a per-dimension style override, since dimension style definitions are outside this block-level projection.
